---
title: "Link between Experiences and Cognition"
format: html
editor: visual
---

```{r}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = F, warning = F, message = F)

library(tidyverse)
library(ggthemes)
library(jsonlite)
library(readxl)
library(tidyboot)
library(ggpubr)
library(ggcorrplot)
library(reshape2)
library(dplyr)
```

```{r load_data}
cognitive_data <- read_csv("../data/cognitive_data.csv")
kcs_rel_data <- read_csv("../data/02_kcs_summary.csv")
kcs_abs_data <- read_csv("../data/02a_global_kcs_summary.csv") |>
  rename(kcs_per_overall = speech_activity_percent)

exposure_rel_data <- read_csv("../data/03_cds_summary.csv")
exposure_abs_data <- read_csv("../data/03a_global_cds_summary.csv")

turn_rel_data <- read_csv("../data/04_turn_taking_summary.csv")
turn_abs_data <- read_csv("../data/04a_global_turn_taking_summary.csv") |>
  rename(turns_per_minute_overall = turns_per_minute)

turn_durations_data <- read_csv("../data/04b_turn_durations_summary.csv")

#frame_composition_data <- read_csv("../data/05_interaction_composition.csv")
frame_composition_data <- read_csv("../data/05_interaction_composition.csv") %>%
  mutate(
    person_or_face_present = if_else(has_face == 1 | has_person == 1, 1, 0)
  )

social_state_data <- read_csv("../data/01_interaction_segments.csv")
```

```{r data_cleaning}
# define affected child IDs
dup_ids <- c(262726, 267567)

# identify rows for those children in the prag task
subset_rows <- cognitive_data$child_id %in% dup_ids & cognitive_data$task == "prag"

# remove duplicates only inside that subset
subset_unique <- unique(cognitive_data[subset_rows, ])

# combine with the untouched rest of the data
cognitive_data_clean <- rbind(
  cognitive_data[!subset_rows, ],
  subset_unique
)

# check result
subset(cognitive_data_clean, child_id %in% dup_ids & task == "prag")

# store clean cognitive data without duplicates 
write.csv(cognitive_data_clean, "../data/cognitive_data_clean.csv", row.names = FALSE)
```

```{r correlation_between_age_dependent_variables}
cognitive_data_clean%>%
  group_by(child_id, task, age)%>%
  summarise(perf = mean(abs(response)))%>%
  filter(!is.na(task))%>%
  ggplot(aes(x = age, y = perf))+
  geom_point(pch = 1)+
  geom_smooth(method = "glm")+
  facet_wrap(~task, scales = "free")+
  #ylim(0,1)+
  labs(x = "Age in years", y = "Performance")+
  theme_bw()
```

```{r generate_distributions_plot}
# 1. Calculate the percentage of successful trials (where response == 1) per child per task
cognitive_data_aggregated <- cognitive_data_clean %>%
  filter(task %in% c("orev", "prag")) %>%
  group_by(child_id, task) %>%
  summarize(
    # Count how many trials equal 1, divide by total trials, and multiply by 100
    score = (sum(response == 1, na.rm = TRUE) / n()) * 100, 
    .groups = "drop"
  )

distribution_plot <- ggplot(cognitive_data_aggregated, aes(x = score, fill = task, color = task)) +
  # 1. Separated density layers with hardcoded light colors
  geom_density(data = filter(cognitive_data_aggregated, task == "orev"), 
               aes(y = after_stat(density) * 35), fill = "#DCDD5B", alpha = 0.5, color = NA) + 
  geom_density(data = filter(cognitive_data_aggregated, task == "prag"), 
               aes(y = after_stat(density) * 35), fill = "#C9D2D5", alpha = 0.5, color = NA) +
  
  # 2. Side-by-side independent dot columns 
  geom_dotplot(aes(group = task), 
               method = "histodot",
               binwidth = 2.5,          
               stackratio = 1.1,        
               dotsize = 0.55,           
               stackgroups = FALSE,      
               binpositions = "bygroup", 
               position = position_dodge(width = 2), 
               alpha = 1,               
               show.legend = TRUE) + # Keeps the single core legend alive
  
  # 3. Scale fill handles the core solid dot colors and the primary legend keys
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("orev" = "#B5B577", "prag" = "#98A3A6"), 
                    labels = c("orev" = "oREV", "prag" = "PRAG")) +
                    
  # 4. CRITICAL FIX: Forces color to use identical mapping but explicitly kills its duplicate legend
  scale_color_manual(values = c("orev" = "#B5B577", "prag" = "#98A3A6"), guide = "none") + 
  
  # Clean bypass override to keep the key markers perfectly solid
  guides(fill = guide_legend(
    override.aes = list(
      alpha = 1,
      color = NA,  # Removes the black outline circle around the legend keys
      size = 10     # Increases the key size so they look prominently bigger
    )
  )) +
  
  xlim(0, 100) + 
  labs(
    title = NULL,
    x = "Success Rate (%)",
    fill = "Domain"  # Naming this 'Domain' unifies any loose aesthetics seamlessly
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 24) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "inside",             
    legend.position.inside = c(0.15, 0.78),
    legend.background = element_rect(fill = "white", color = NA), 
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    axis.text.y = element_blank(), 
    axis.title.y = element_blank(),
    # --- UPDATED LAYOUT ADJUSTMENTS FOR MAXIMUM COMPACTNESS ---
    legend.title = element_text(margin = margin(b = 1)),        
    legend.key.height = unit(0.05, "cm"),                           
    legend.margin = margin(t = 2, r = 5, b = 2, l = 5, unit = "pt") 
  )

distribution_plot
```

```{r generate_correlation_plot}
# --- PANEL B: DOMAIN CORRELATION ---

# 2. Pivot to a wide format for the correlation and scatter plot steps
wide_cognitive_data <- cognitive_data_aggregated %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = task, values_from = score) %>%
  drop_na(orev, prag) 

# 3. Calculate statistical correlation
domain_correlation <- cor.test(wide_cognitive_data$orev, wide_cognitive_data$prag, method = "pearson")

r_val <- round(domain_correlation$estimate, 2)
p_val <- round(domain_correlation$p.value, 4)

correlation_plot <- ggplot(wide_cognitive_data, aes(x = orev, y = prag)) +
  geom_point(color = "#BEA468", alpha = 1, size = 2.9) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", formula = y ~ x, color = "#927B2D", fill = "#E0D2B6", se = TRUE) +
  xlim(0, 100) + # Enforces percentage bounds
  ylim(0, 100) +
  annotate("label", 
           x = 2, 
           y = 97.5, 
           label = paste0("r = ", r_val, "\np ", ifelse(p_val < 0.001, "< .001", paste0("= ", p_val))),
           fontface = "italic", 
           size = 24 / 2.845, 
           hjust = 0,
           vjust = 1,               # Anchors the text box neatly from the top-left corner
           fill = "white",            # Makes the background solid white
           label.size = 0) +
  labs(
    title = NULL,
    x = "oREV Success Rate (%)",
    y = "PRAG Success Rate (%)",
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 24) +
  theme(
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0)
  )
```

```{r grenerate_combined_plot}
combined_plot <- plot_grid(
  distribution_plot,
  correlation_plot,
  nrow = 1,
  align = "h",                          
  rel_widths = c(1, 0.9),                                
  labels = c("A", "B"), 
  label_fontface = "bold",
  label_x = c(0.01, -0.015),  # Pulls 'B' out into the empty whitespace on the left
  hjust = 0                 # Keeps them neatly left-aligned at their new coordinates
)

ggsave("../outputs/domain_descriptive_plots.png", combined_plot, width = 20, height = 6, dpi = 600, scale = 0.80)
```

```{r correlation_between_cognitive_tests}
cognitive_data_clean%>%
  group_by(child_id, task)%>%
  summarise(perf = mean(abs(response)))%>%
  filter(!is.na(task))%>%
  left_join(cognitive_data_clean %>%
              rename("task2"= task)%>%
              filter(!is.na(task2))%>%
              group_by(child_id, task2)%>%
              summarise(perf2 = mean(abs(response))))%>%
  filter(task != task2)%>%
  rowwise()%>%
  mutate(tid = paste(sort(c(task, task2)), collapse = " - "))%>%
  ungroup()%>%
  distinct(child_id,tid, .keep_all = T)%>%
  ggplot(aes(x = perf, y = perf2))+
  #geom_abline(intercept = 0, slope = 1, lty = 3, alpha = .75)+
  geom_smooth(method = "lm")+
  geom_point(pch = 1)+
  facet_wrap(task ~ task2, scales = "free")+
  stat_cor()+
  theme_few()
```

```{r prepare_cognitive_data}
# Aggregate Cognitive Data
cog_summary <- cognitive_data_clean %>%
  group_by(child_id, sex, task) %>% 
  summarise(
    age_at_test = min(as.numeric(age), na.rm = TRUE),
    
    # Logic for Count-based tasks (OREV, PRAG)
    successes = if(first(task) %in% c("orev", "prag")) {
                  sum(abs(as.numeric(response)), na.rm = TRUE) 
                } else { NA },
    
    trials = n(),
    
    # Logic for Distance-based tasks (TANGO)
    avg_error = if(first(task) == "tango") {
                  mean(abs(as.numeric(response)), na.rm = TRUE) 
                } else { NA },
    
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  # Pivot wider to get one column per task-metric pair
  pivot_wider(
    id_cols = c(child_id, age_at_test, sex),
    names_from = task,
    values_from = c(successes, trials, avg_error),
    names_glue = "{task}_{.value}"
  ) %>%
  # Clean up the column names to be more intuitive
  rename(
    tango_error = tango_avg_error
  ) %>%
  # Remove columns that are entirely NA (like orev_avg_error or tango_successes)
  select(where(~ !all(is.na(.))))
```

```{r prepare_kcs_data}
# --- Step 2: Weighted Aggregation with Global Min Age ---
kcs_rel_wide <- kcs_rel_data %>%
  # 1. First, create a global minimum age for each child across ALL states
  group_by(child_id) %>%
  mutate(age_at_recording = min(age_at_recording, na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  
  # 2. Group by child, that global age, and state to aggregate speech
  group_by(child_id, age_at_recording, interaction_type) %>%
  summarize(
    total_segment_minutes = sum(segment_duration_minutes, na.rm = TRUE),
    total_speech_minutes = sum(kchi_speech_minutes, na.rm = TRUE),
    # Weighted activity percentage
    total_speech_activity = total_speech_minutes / total_segment_minutes,
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  
  # 3. Pivot to wide format
  select(child_id, age_at_recording, interaction_type, total_speech_activity) %>%
  pivot_wider(
    names_from = interaction_type, 
    values_from = total_speech_activity,
    names_prefix = "kcs_per_" 
  )
```

```{r prepare_exposure_data}
# Define a helper to process the different exposure types
process_exposure <- function(data, prefix) {
  data %>%
    group_by(child_id) %>%
    mutate(age_at_recording = min(age_at_recording, na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
    group_by(child_id, age_at_recording, interaction_type) %>%
    summarize(
      total_segment_sec = sum(total_segment_duration, na.rm = TRUE),
      total_speech_sec = sum(total_speech_seconds, na.rm = TRUE),
      # Ensure units match for the proportion
      activity_prop = total_speech_sec / total_segment_sec,
      .groups = "drop"
    ) %>%
    select(child_id, age_at_recording, interaction_type, activity_prop) %>%
    pivot_wider(
      names_from = interaction_type, 
      values_from = activity_prop,
      names_prefix = paste0(prefix, "_per_")
    )
}

# 2. Create three tables
total_exposure_rel_wide <- process_exposure(exposure_rel_data %>% filter(exposure_type == "TOTAL"), "total_speech_exposure")
kcds_exposure_rel_wide  <- process_exposure(exposure_rel_data %>% filter(exposure_type == "KCDS_ONLY"), "kcds_exposure")
ohs_exposure_rel_wide   <- process_exposure(exposure_rel_data %>% filter(exposure_type == "OHS_ONLY"), "ohs_exposure")

total_exposure_abs_wide <- exposure_abs_data %>% 
  filter(exposure_type == "TOTAL") %>% 
  rename(total_speech_exposure_per_overall = exposure_percent)

kcds_exposure_abs_wide <- exposure_abs_data %>% 
  filter(exposure_type == "KCDS_ONLY") %>% 
  rename(kcds_exposure_per_overall = exposure_percent)

ohs_exposure_abs_wide <- exposure_abs_data %>% 
  filter(exposure_type == "OHS_ONLY") %>% 
  rename(ohs_exposure_per_overall = exposure_percent)
```

```{r prepare_turn_data}
# Process for plotting points: ONE point per child
turn_rel_wide <- turn_rel_data %>%
  group_by(child_id) %>%
  summarize(
    age_at_recording = min(age_at_recording),
    total_segment_minutes = sum(segment_duration_minutes),
    total_turns_sum = sum(total_turns),
    # Weighted average turns per minute
    turns_per_minute_interacting = total_turns_sum / total_segment_minutes,
    .groups = "drop"
  )
```

```{r prepare_agency_data}
# --- Step 2: Calculate Weighted Agency (Initiation only) ---
turns_agency_wide <- turn_rel_data %>%
  mutate(
    total_successful_blocks = successful_initiations + successful_responses
  ) %>%
  # 1. Filter out zero-activity segments before aggregating
  filter(total_successful_blocks > 0) %>%
  group_by(child_id) %>%
  summarize(
    # Keep the global minimum recording age
    age_at_recording = min(age_at_recording, na.rm = TRUE),
    # Sum the raw counts to create a weighted ratio
    total_init = sum(successful_initiations, na.rm = TRUE),
    total_blocks = sum(total_successful_blocks, na.rm = TRUE),
    # Calculate the single Agency value
    agency_initiation_prop = total_init / total_blocks,
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  # 3. Select only the ID and the new metric for the join
  select(child_id, agency_initiation_prop)
```

```{r prepare_turn_duration_data}
turn_durations_wide <- turn_durations_data %>%
  group_by(child_id) %>%
  summarize(
    # Get the raw age for the x-axis
    age_at_recording = min(as.numeric(age_at_recording), na.rm = TRUE),
    # Calculate the average duration for this child
    avg_turn_duration = mean(vocalization_duration_sec, na.rm = TRUE),
    total_vocalizations = n(),
    .groups = "drop"
  )
```

```{r prepare_block_duration_data}
block_durations_wide <- turn_rel_data %>%
  group_by(child_id) %>%
  summarize(
    # Get the raw age for the x-axis
    age_at_recording = min(as.numeric(age_at_recording), na.rm = TRUE),
    # Calculate the average duration for this child
    avg_block_duration = mean(block_duration_sec, na.rm = TRUE),
    # Total blocks analyzed for weighting in future models
    total_blocks_count = n(),
    .groups = "drop"
  )
```

```{r prepare_turns_per_block}
turns_per_block <- turn_rel_data %>%
  group_by(child_id) %>%
  summarise(
    # Total turns in all successful blocks / Total number of successful blocks
    avg_turns_per_block = sum(total_turns) / sum(successful_initiations + successful_responses),
    .groups = "drop"
  )
```

```{r prepare_frame_level_data}
frame_composition_summary <- frame_composition_data %>%
  group_by(child_id) %>%
  summarize(
    # Visual Environment (%)
    pct_face_visible = mean(has_face, na.rm = TRUE),
    pct_person_visible = mean(has_person, na.rm = TRUE),
    pct_face_or_person_visible = mean(person_or_face_present, na.rm = TRUE),
    
    # Physical Proximity (Average Score)
    avg_face_proximity = mean(proximity_filled[proximity_filled != -1], na.rm = TRUE),
    age_at_recording = min(age_at_recording, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )
```

```{r prepare_social_state_data}
# Process for plotting points: One value per child per social state
social_state_wide <- social_state_data %>%
  group_by(child_id) %>%
  mutate(
    child_total_sec = sum(duration_sec, na.rm = TRUE),
    min_age_rec = min(age_at_recording, na.rm = TRUE)
  ) %>%
  
  # 2. Group by child, their global min age, and state
  group_by(child_id, min_age_rec, interaction_type) %>%
  summarise(
    prop = sum(duration_sec) / max(child_total_sec),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  
  # 3. Pivot to wide format
  pivot_wider(
    names_from = interaction_type, 
    values_from = prop,
    names_prefix = "pct_"
  )
```

```{r combine_data}
# --- STEP 3: Combine Everything ---
behavior_iv_df <- kcs_abs_data %>% 
              select(child_id, kcs_per_overall) %>%
  
  left_join(kcs_rel_wide %>% 
              select(child_id, age_at_recording, starts_with("kcs_per_")), by = "child_id"
            ) %>%
  
  left_join(total_exposure_abs_wide %>% 
              select(child_id, total_speech_exposure_per_overall), by = "child_id"
            ) %>%
  
  left_join(kcds_exposure_abs_wide %>% 
              select(child_id, kcds_exposure_per_overall), by = "child_id"
            ) %>%
  
  left_join(ohs_exposure_abs_wide %>% 
              select(child_id, ohs_exposure_per_overall), by = "child_id"
            ) %>%
  
  left_join(total_exposure_rel_wide %>% 
              select(child_id, starts_with("total_speech_exposure_per_")), 
              by = "child_id"
              ) %>%
  
  left_join(kcds_exposure_rel_wide %>% 
              select(child_id, starts_with("kcds_exposure_per_")), by = "child_id"
            ) %>%
  
  left_join(ohs_exposure_rel_wide %>% 
              select(child_id, starts_with("ohs_exposure_per_")), 
            by = "child_id"
            ) %>%
  
  left_join(turn_rel_wide %>% 
              select(child_id, turns_per_minute_interacting), 
            by = "child_id"
            ) %>%
  
  left_join(turn_abs_data %>% 
              select(child_id, turns_per_minute_overall), 
            by = "child_id"
            ) %>%
  left_join(turns_agency_wide,
            by = "child_id"
            ) %>%
  left_join(turn_durations_wide %>%
              select(child_id, avg_turn_duration),
            by = "child_id"
            ) %>%
  left_join(block_durations_wide %>%
              select(child_id, avg_block_duration),
            by = "child_id"
            ) %>%
  left_join(turns_per_block %>% 
              select(child_id, avg_turns_per_block),
            by = "child_id"
            ) %>%
  left_join(frame_composition_summary %>%
              select(child_id, pct_face_visible, pct_person_visible, 
                     pct_face_or_person_visible, avg_face_proximity),
            by = "child_id"
            ) %>%
  left_join(social_state_wide, by = "child_id")
  

# Check which children are missing behavioral features
print(length(unique(cog_summary$child_id)))
missing_ivs <- anti_join(cog_summary, behavior_iv_df, by = "child_id")
print(nrow(missing_ivs))

# Combine behavior_iv_df and cog_summary for final df (only keep children that are in both dataset)
final_df = cog_summary %>% inner_join(behavior_iv_df, by = "child_id")
colnames(final_df)
# save final_df
write_csv(final_df, "../data/final_feature_df.csv")
```

```{r generate_matrix}
# 1. Define your Independent Variables
iv_list <- c(
  "kcs_per_overall", 
  "kcs_per_Interacting", "kcs_per_Not_Interacting",
  "total_speech_exposure_per_overall", "total_speech_exposure_per_Interacting", "total_speech_exposure_per_Not_Interacting",
  "kcds_exposure_per_overall", "kcds_exposure_per_Interacting", "kcds_exposure_per_Not_Interacting",
  "ohs_exposure_per_overall", "ohs_exposure_per_Interacting", "ohs_exposure_per_Not_Interacting",
  "turns_per_minute_overall",
  "turns_per_minute_interacting",
  "agency_initiation_prop",
  "avg_turn_duration",
  "avg_block_duration",
  "avg_turns_per_block",
  "pct_face_visible", "pct_person_visible", "pct_face_or_person_visible", "avg_face_proximity",
  "pct_Interacting", "pct_Not_Interacting"
)

# 2. Convert to numeric and THEN run correlation
iv_cor_matrix <- final_df %>%
  select(any_of(iv_list)) %>% 
  # This line fixes the 'x must be numeric' error
  mutate(across(everything(), ~as.numeric(as.character(.)))) %>%
  cor(use = "pairwise.complete.obs", method = "pearson")

# 3. Plotting code remains the same
melted_iv_cor <- melt(iv_cor_matrix)

# Mask the lower triangle and diagonal
#iv_cor_matrix[lower.tri(iv_cor_matrix, diag = TRUE)] <- NA
#melted_iv_cor <- melt(iv_cor_matrix, na.rm = TRUE)

ggplot(melted_iv_cor, aes(Var1, Var2, fill = value)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white") +
  # Shrink the correlation numbers
  geom_text(aes(label = round(value, 2)), size = 3) + 
  scale_fill_gradient2(low = "#2166ac", high = "#b2182b", mid = "white", 
                       limit = c(-1, 1), name = "Pearson\nr") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    # Shrink and angle the axis labels
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, vjust = 1, hjust = 1, size = 8),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 8),
    axis.title = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major = element_blank()
  ) +
  # This forces the tiles to be perfect squares
  coord_fixed()

ggsave("../outputs/predictor_matrix_large.png", width = 14, height = 12, dpi = 300)
```
